channel.title: Linux on Perfect Dark Mode
channel.description: Recent content in Linux on Perfect Dark Mode

item.title: Fedora Desktop Setup
item.description: When you first download Fedora Workstation, it’s going to be a little hard to figure out how to make it usable. Especially if you’ve never tinkered with Linux before. Here is how to set up Fedora. Add badname user $ adduser --badname firstname.lastname $ sudo usermod -aG wheel username # uncomment this line in the visudo file $ sudo visudo %wheel ALL=(ALL) ALLDelete other user: $ userdel username DNF Config (for speed) DNF is one of the package managers that comes with Fedora.

item.title: RHCSA Lab Using Vagrant
item.description: We are going to use Vagrant to set up two RHEL 8 servers with some custom configuration options. I will include some helpful Vagrant commands at the end if you get stuck. In this guide, I will be using Fedora 38 as my main operating system. I use Fedora because it is similar in features to Red Hat Linux Distributions. This will give me even more practice for the RHCSA exam as I use it in day-to-day operations.

item.title: RHCSA Notes
item.description: Here are my notes from Asghar Gori’s RHCSA book. Buy the book or read the reviews here. Chapter 1: Installation [Chapter 2:rhcsa/2_interaction.md [Chapter 3:rhcsa/3_file_management.md [Chapter 4:rhcsa/4_adv_file_management.md [Chapter 5:rhcsa/5_basic_user_management.md [Chapter 1:rhcsa/6_Adv_user_management.md [Chapter 1:rhcsa/7_bash_shell.md [Chapter 1:rhcsa/8_process_and_task_scheduling.md [Chapter 1:rhcsa/9_basic_package_management.md [Chapter 1:rhcsa/16_networking.md [Chapter 1:rhcsa/17_Network_File_System.md
